name: Regimen A — Repurposing backbone (pharmacy-accessible)
philosophy: All-generic, all oral, BBB-permeable. Simultaneously dampens NF-kB survival, PI3K/mTOR growth, metabolic/autophagy escape. Lowest access barrier; can be layered onto standard care with oncologist supervision.
precedent: CUSP9* protocol (NCT02770378) — feasibility/safety demonstrated; DIRECT disulfiram trial (NCT02678975)
gaps: Does NOT directly hit p53-null G2/M dependency (V2) or telomere axis (V1). Cytostatic-leaning.

name: Regimen B — Targeted synthetic-lethal core (needs onc/trial access)
philosophy: Exploits the truncal p53 loss: with G1/S checkpoint gone, force cells through mitosis with unrepaired damage. Pair a brain-penetrant PI3K/mTOR inhibitor with a G2/M-checkpoint inhibitor.
precedent: Paxalisib GBM AGILE (NCT03970447); AZD1775/adavosertib GBM Phase 0/1 (NCT02207010, NCT01849146)
gaps: Access-limited; overlapping myelosuppression risk requires careful dosing.

name: Regimen C — Integrated (backbone + one targeted anchor)
philosophy: Realistic 'best of both': the generic backbone for broad pathway suppression PLUS one accessible targeted anchor (metformin+paxalisib+ketogenic metabolic pressure), mirroring an actual trial design.
precedent: Paxalisib + ketogenic diet + metformin trial (NCT05183204) — direct real-world analog of this exact combination
gaps: Drug-drug interaction & tolerability load; requires active oncologist management.
